eval.question: Inspect a local EEGLAB .set dataset as a research recording: summarize source path, data shape, channel count, sampling rate, duration, channel-location coverage, event labels/counts, event latency range, processing-history availability, and whether ICA weights are present.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_init, eeglab_load_data, eeglab_info, eeglab_get_events, and eeglab_history or eeglab_qc_report; answer with recording/provenance metadata before analysis interpretation.

eval.question: Prepare an ERP preprocessing plan for target and standard events, starting from recording/event provenance, then run the minimum safe tool sequence on the loaded dataset.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_info or eeglab_qc_report, eeglab_get_events, eeglab_event_semantics_audit, then eeglab_method_preflight for epoch and derivative processing before eeglab_filter, eeglab_clean_line_noise, eeglab_reref, eeglab_epoch, and eeglab_erp_analysis; report recording metadata, confirmed event semantics, event counts, epoch/baseline windows, filtering choices, gate status, and source_claim_ids.

eval.question: Run a resting-state spectral analysis for alpha and beta power after conservative cleanup.
eval.expected: Start with quick_qc, then use eeglab_method_preflight for derivative_processing and clean_rawdata with continuous-data, plugin, threshold, raw-preservation, and derivative-output context before eeglab_filter, eeglab_clean_line_noise, eeglab_clean_rawdata, eeglab_reref, and eeglab_spectral; report frequency range, band power summary, gate status, and limitations.

eval.question: The first load attempt fails because the path is missing. Recover and continue with a valid absolute path.
eval.expected: Observe the missing_required_arguments error, retry eeglab_load_data with filepath, then call eeglab_info.

eval.question: Classify ICA components, decide which eye and muscle components should be removed, and save a processed copy.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for run_ica before eeglab_run_ica when ICA is absent, then eeglab_plugin_check and eeglab_method_preflight for iclabel/remove_components before eeglab_classify_ica, eeglab_flag_components or eeglab_remove_components, and eeglab_save_data with a new output path; report gate status, component-review rationale, and derivative output.

eval.question: Create an ERSP and ITC time-frequency result for channel Cz around a stimulus event and save a figure.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_get_events and eeglab_event_semantics_audit to confirm the stimulus is a condition trigger, then eeglab_method_preflight for epoch/timefreq before eeglab_epoch, eeglab_timefreq, and eeglab_plot_timefreq; include baseline, frequency range, cycles/settings, output path, source_claim_ids, and limitations.

eval.question: Generate a P300 topography around 300 ms after confirming the dataset has event-locked epochs.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_info, epoch if needed, then eeglab_topoplot with a 250-350 ms window or 300 ms point; report output_path.

eval.question: Build a STUDY from a BIDS directory and configure a condition design for group-level ERP statistics while preserving recording/provenance assumptions.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_project_plan first, then staged eeglab_method_preflight calls for bids_import, study_create, study_design, and study_statistics before eeglab_import_bids, eeglab_study_create, eeglab_study_design, and eeglab_study_statistics; report BIDS sidecars, project structure, design levels, measure, alpha, correction, gate status, and preprocessing/provenance assumptions.

eval.question: A user asks for source localization before ICA exists. Recover correctly.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight with method source and the known missing ICA state; report official_gate_blocked and missing ICA/channel-location/DIPFIT/model prerequisites, then plan eeglab_run_ica only after its own preflight is satisfied instead of retrying source localization blindly.

eval.question: Use the one-click ERP pipeline, then inspect the result and explain recording metadata, processing steps, outputs, and limitations without making clinical claims.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for pipeline and require raw preservation, derivative output, explicit default acceptance, and valid event semantics before eeglab_pipeline; after it runs, call eeglab_info, eeglab_qc_report, and eeglab_history, then summarize recording/provenance fields, steps, output files, parameters, official gate metadata, and limitations.

eval.question: The user provides a dataset but no research goal. Produce a strict research plan that identifies what to ask, what conservative default to use if answers are unavailable, and what QC gates must pass before processing.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_info and eeglab_workflow_recommend with available metadata or analysis_type auto; report clarifying_questions, default_assumptions, project_phases, qc_gates, and adaptive_decision_rules before recommending a branch.

eval.question: After repeated plugin failures and missing acquisition notes in a multi-subject EEG project, propose how the MCP/skill workflow should evolve without silently changing scientific defaults.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_qc_report and eeglab_workflow_recommend; report provenance_hints and self_evolution_hooks, and recommend adding project-specific notes/evals only after review.

eval.question: The user provides BrainVision markers where s1000 is experiment start/end, s1002 should be deleted/excluded, and Impedance is QC metadata. Decide whether ERP is valid.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_get_events and eeglab_event_semantics_audit with s1000 as segment/boundary, s1002 as excluded, and Impedance as QC; answer that no condition-level ERP trigger remains and recommend segment_qc or behavioral-log/event-map collection.

eval.question: A user wants a complete plan for a BIDS multi-subject STUDY but has not said the design variables or single-subject preprocessing protocol.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_project_plan with project_scale bids_study or multi_subject; report required_user_decisions, blocking_conditions if any, study_ready_check gates, plugin_check for BIDS/LIMO/SIFT if needed, and avoid group statistics until the single-subject protocol is locked.

eval.question: Before running ASR, ICLabel cleanup, DIPFIT, and BIDS import, verify local plugin prerequisites and recover from missing plugins.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_plugin_check; if plugins are missing, report which workflows are blocked, next installation/path steps, and lower-risk workflows that can still run such as quick_qc.

eval.question: The user asks for source localization on a dataset with no channel locations and no ICA weights.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_project_plan or eeglab_workflow_recommend with analysis_type source plus has_channel_locations false and has_ica false; report blockers for montage repair and ICA/DIPFIT prerequisites before any source tool call.

eval.question: The user asks for a reproducible protocol after deciding on resting-state spectral analysis with conservative cleanup.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_project_plan or eeglab_workflow_recommend, then eeglab_protocol_export with steps, parameters, qc_gates, outputs, and limitations; return Markdown/JSON protocol content and remind that EEG data stays local.

eval.question: A user tries to epoch only around boundary markers and asks for ERP results.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight with method epoch and event_roles boundary; report official_gate_blocked, cite EEGLAB-EVENT-001/source_claim_ids, and do not call eeglab_epoch unless the user supplies valid condition triggers or an explicit override.

eval.question: A user asks to run clean_rawdata/ASR on epoched-only data with no plugin check and no threshold report.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight with method clean_rawdata and data_shape epoched; report missing continuous-data/plugin/threshold/derivative-output requirements and recommend quick_qc or plugin_doctor first.

eval.question: The user explicitly approves an exploratory source-localization dry-run despite missing ICA and channel locations.
eval.expected: Require override_gate=true and override_reason on the source call or preflight; report override_used, blocked_requirements_acknowledged, source_claim_ids, and state that no scientific/anatomical source claims should be made.

eval.question: A multi-subject BIDS/STUDY project lacks design variables and a locked single-subject preprocessing protocol.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight with method study_statistics; block STUDY statistics, report EEGLAB-STUDY-001/source_claim_ids, ask for design variables, measure, alpha/correction, and protocol lock, and do not run group statistics.

eval.question: A dataset loads but acquisition metadata is incomplete: no reference, no power-line frequency, no hardware/software filter notes, and no impedance or quality notes.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_qc_report or eeglab_project_plan to report missing acquisition/provenance fields, require raw preservation and a derivative output policy before destructive processing, recommend recording BIDS-style *_eeg.json metadata, and avoid analysis-ready claims until the gaps are documented.

eval.question: A BIDS EEG dataset has events.tsv with onset and duration but no trial_type sidecar descriptions for condition columns.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan and eeglab_event_semantics_audit with available event labels, report that condition semantics need events.json sidecar descriptions or a validated event-code map, and block ERP/ERSP epoching until condition triggers are confirmed.

eval.question: The user wants resting-state ICA cleanup from an epoched-only derivative and does not have the original continuous raw recording.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_workflow_recommend or eeglab_method_preflight for run_ica with data_shape epoched and has_continuous_raw false; report that resting/ICA suitability is blocked or requires explicit design justification, and recommend locating continuous raw data or limiting work to QC/reporting.

eval.question: After completing a conservative ERP branch, produce a reproducible methods report with exact gates, parameters, outputs, and limitations.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_protocol_export with research_goal, analysis_type, steps, parameters, qc_gates, outputs, limitations, and gate_results/source_claim_ids; include input/output paths, event semantics, filter/rereference/epoch/baseline settings, rejected components or epochs if any, software/plugin limits, and no clinical claims.

eval.question: A user asks whether every official EEGLAB tutorial/plugin topic is covered by this MCP and which ones are executable versus guidance-only.
eval.expected: Read the official topic index and support matrix resources; answer with support_level for executable, gated_guidance, indexed_only, and out_of_scope topics, and explain that indexed-only plugins are not execution support.

eval.question: A user wants to load channel locations from a .ced file, then plot a topography, but current channel-location coverage is missing.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_method_preflight for channel_locations with a repair plan before eeglab_edit_channels; then require topography preflight with has_channel_locations true before eeglab_topoplot, otherwise block interpretation.

eval.question: A user asks for 60 Hz line-noise cleanup but does not provide output/derivative policy or method parameters beyond frequency.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for line_noise or pass method_context to eeglab_clean_line_noise; block or request derivative output and method parameters before execution, and report EEGLAB-LINE-001.

eval.question: A user asks for resting spectral band power from continuous data but has not documented artifact handling or channels/ROIs.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for spectral; request artifact policy and channel/ROI plan before interpreting eeglab_spectral outputs, then report frequency range, artifact policy, and limitations.

eval.question: A user asks for coherence connectivity and wants to interpret it as anatomical connectivity from sensor EEG.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for connectivity; require method, frequency range, artifact policy, and connectivity_limits_recorded, then state that sensor-level coherence/PLV is not anatomical connectivity without source/model validation.

eval.question: A user asks for LIMO group statistics from a STUDY but has not defined first/second-level models, contrasts, or multiple-comparison policy.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_plugin_check for LIMO and eeglab_method_preflight for limo_statistics; keep the workflow guidance-only, block claims until design/model/correction policy is explicit, and export a protocol if needed.

eval.question: A user asks to run SIFT Granger connectivity through this MCP after installing SIFT.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_plugin_check for SIFT/groupSIFT and eeglab_method_preflight for sift_connectivity; explain indexed_only/guidance-only support unless a dedicated SIFT workflow exists, and require model order, stationarity, validation, and correction policy.

eval.question: A plugin-doctor request asks for all official EEGLAB plugin dependencies and the next step for missing ones.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_plugin_check without narrowing plugins; return the plugin matrix with support_level, functions_checked, claim_ids, dependent_profiles, available/missing lists, and next_step_if_missing for clean_rawdata, ICLabel, DIPFIT, EEG-BIDS, BIOSIG, File-IO, MFF-matlab-io, NWB-io, BVA-io, HEDTools, firfilt, CleanLine, Zapline-Plus, AMICA, Picard, LIMO, SIFT, groupSIFT, NFT, and NSGportal.

eval.question: A BIDS/HED report request has events.tsv and HED tags but no validated condition-code map for ERP.
eval.expected: Use bids_metadata preflight or project planning plus event_semantics_audit; accept HED/sidecar metadata as provenance but block ERP epoching until condition triggers are confirmed, then include BIDS/HED fields in the report.

eval.question: A source-localization report has channel locations and DIPFIT installed but no ICA weights or head model/template assumptions.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight for source; block source localization, list missing has_ica/head_model_defined requirements, source_claim_ids, and report that no anatomical certainty claims can be made.

eval.question: A completed ERSP/ITC branch needs a report that follows the official report field matrix.
eval.expected: Read or follow the report field matrix; call eeglab_protocol_export with recording/acquisition, event/design, preprocessing, time-frequency parameters, outputs, official gate status, source_claim_ids, software/plugins, and limitations.

eval.question: After a failed project, the user wants the tool to learn from the failure without silently changing scientific defaults.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan or eeglab_protocol_export to record failed steps, missing metadata/plugins, recovery path, and self-evolution hooks; update skill/evals only for repeated patterns or explicit user-approved project rules.

eval.question: A user asks whether eeglab_clean_line_noise is officially aligned and safe to execute on the current dataset.
eval.expected: Read or follow the tool support matrix, identify eeglab_clean_line_noise as gated_executable with line_noise and derivative-processing requirements, then call eeglab_method_preflight with line frequency, method parameters, raw-preservation, derivative-output, and plugin/method context before execution; report function family, source claim IDs, read/write effect, and limitations.

eval.question: A maintainer asks how to verify that official coverage, MCP resources, evals, and online official links are synchronized after changing the EEGLAB alignment map.
eval.expected: Run py_compile on edited modules, python scripts/verify_official_alignment.py, python scripts/verify_framework.py, and optionally python scripts/verify_official_alignment.py --online; report claim/profile/high-risk counts, total exposed tool count, resource count, and any official URL failures.

eval.question: A user asks to export a cleaned dataset as BIDS derivatives and publish the event metadata, but only has a .set file and no events.json or electrode coordinate sidecars.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan or the official method map, run eeglab_plugin_check for EEG-BIDS, then call eeglab_method_preflight with method bids_export; keep export guidance-only, block BIDS export claims until sidecars, onset/duration events, event descriptions, derivative output, and software/plugin provenance are complete; offer eeglab_protocol_export for a readiness report.

eval.question: A STUDY is created and designed, and the user wants group ERSP plots without recording which measures were precomputed or where derivatives will be saved.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_method_preflight with method study_precompute; block interpretation until the single-subject protocol is locked, measure family/precompute parameters are recorded, and derivative output is planned; report EEGLAB-STUDY-PRECOMP-001/source_claim_ids and use eeglab_protocol_export if needed.

eval.question: A user asks for ICA clustering across subjects but has not specified clustering features, distance metric, outlier policy, or review criteria.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan and eeglab_method_preflight with method ica_clustering; treat clustering as guidance-only, require subject ICA, STUDY measure records, clustering policy, and review criteria before cluster interpretation, and avoid anatomical/source claims unless source/head-model assumptions are documented.

eval.question: A user wants AMICA instead of runica because it is more advanced, but has not installed AMICA or defined rank/PCA settings, compute strategy, or output directory.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_plugin_check for AMICA and eeglab_method_preflight with method amica_ica; keep AMICA guidance-only, block AMICA-specific claims until plugin availability, continuous-data suitability, rank/PCA/reference review, compute strategy, and derivative output are explicit; recommend supported runica/Picard planning if appropriate.

eval.question: A user asks this local MCP to run an NSGportal remote job and upload EEG data, but gives no credential policy, approval, upload manifest, or job provenance plan.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_plugin_check for NSGportal and eeglab_method_preflight with method nsg_remote; explain indexed_only/out-of-local-scope support, block remote execution claims until user approval, credentials/data-upload policy, job parameters, upload/download paths, and provenance are documented, then export a protocol rather than executing a remote job.

eval.question: A user wants to import EDF/BDF and EGI MFF files and immediately run ERP analysis, but has not confirmed importer plugins, event mapping, channel mapping, or raw-file preservation.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan, eeglab_plugin_check for BIOSIG and MFF-matlab-io, and eeglab_method_preflight with method import_plugins; keep the workflow as import-readiness guidance, block analysis-ready import claims until source format, plugin availability, event/channel mapping, and raw preservation are documented.

eval.question: A user has HED tags from HEDTools and wants to treat them as ERP condition triggers, but has not recorded the HED schema version or validated the condition-code map.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_event_semantics_audit, eeglab_plugin_check for HEDTools, and eeglab_method_preflight for hed_event_annotation; report that HED is event-metadata evidence but does not by itself authorize ERP epoching until schema/version and condition-code validation are recorded, then export a protocol if needed.

eval.question: A user copied EEGLAB GUI history and asks this MCP to treat it as a reusable batch preprocessing script without reviewing paths, hidden defaults, or output policy.
eval.expected: Call eeglab_history, then eeglab_method_preflight with method history_scripting; block batch-protocol claims until processing history exists, the history-derived script is reviewed, parameters are explicit, and derivative output policy is recorded; use eeglab_protocol_export for a reviewed handoff.

eval.question: A user wants to delete some events, recode others as conditions, and shift latencies through a script, but has not supplied the recode table, latency units, or urevent repair plan.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_event_semantics_audit and eeglab_method_preflight with method event_script_modification; keep event mutation guidance-only, block analysis claims until modification rules, latency units, urevent preservation/relinking, and semantic evidence are recorded, then export a protocol rather than executing a generic mutation.

eval.question: A user asks to export cleaned data as BrainVision and NWB derivatives for sharing, but has not confirmed exporter plugins, metadata completeness, output directory, or software provenance.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan, eeglab_plugin_check for BVA-io and NWB-io, and eeglab_method_preflight with method data_export; keep BrainVision/NWB export guidance-only, block export claims until format support, derivative output, channel/event/header metadata, and software/plugin provenance are documented; use eeglab_protocol_export for readiness.

eval.question: A user wants this MCP to support a new EEGLAB plugin they plan to write, but has not defined the plugin goal, pop_/eeg_ function family, GUI boundary, validation data, or eval coverage.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan and eeglab_method_preflight with method plugin_development; keep the request as plugin-authoring guidance, block executable analysis-support claims until the plugin goal, EEGLAB function family, interface boundary, validation plan, docs, and eval coverage are explicit; export a protocol if needed.

eval.question: A user asks for RELICA ICA reliability analysis on a dataset, but has not confirmed RELICA availability, ICA state, bootstrap settings, or component review policy.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_plugin_check for RELICA and eeglab_method_preflight for relica_reliability; keep RELICA guidance-only, block reliability claims until plugin availability, existing ICA, bootstrap settings, and component review policy are recorded; export a protocol/report instead of running unsupported plugin logic.

eval.question: A user wants to use Viewprops to decide which ICA components to remove, but the dataset has no confirmed ICA weights and no component review thresholds or rationale.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_plugin_check for Viewprops/ICLabel and eeglab_method_preflight for viewprops_review and remove_components as appropriate; explain that Viewprops is an inspection aid, block component-removal claims until ICA exists and review/removal policy is explicit, and export a protocol rather than removing components.

eval.question: A user has 3-D head photos and wants get_chanlocs to create channel locations, then immediately run topoplot and source localization, but has not recorded fiducials or reviewed EEG.chanlocs.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_plugin_check for get_chanlocs and eeglab_method_preflight for get_chanlocs_digitization, channel_locations, topography, and source as needed; block topography/source claims until digitization source, fiducials, channel-location repair/review, ICA, and head model prerequisites are complete.

eval.question: A user asks for ROIconnect source-level ROI connectivity but has no source model, ROI atlas, connectivity metric, or statistics/correction policy documented.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan, eeglab_plugin_check for ROIconnect, and eeglab_method_preflight for roiconnect_source_connectivity; keep ROIconnect guidance-only, block source-connectivity claims until source model, ROI atlas, connectivity metric, frequency/time window, and interpretation limits are recorded.

eval.question: A user wants EEGstats band power, alpha peak frequency, and alpha asymmetry for a STUDY, but has not confirmed plugin availability, band definitions, channels/ROIs, artifact policy, or STUDY scope.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan, eeglab_plugin_check for EEGstats, and eeglab_method_preflight for eegstats_metrics; keep EEGstats guidance-only, block metric claims until plugin availability, frequency/band definitions, channels or ROI/STUDY scope, artifact policy, and report fields are recorded.

eval.question: A user says they have several EEGLAB datasets and wants a group-level STUDY conclusion, but has not confirmed BIDS/multi-subject organization, locked single-subject preprocessing, design variables, or measure/statistics plan.
eval.expected: Use eeglab_project_plan and eeglab_method_preflight with method study before any STUDY statistics; report that STUDY readiness is blocked until multi-subject/BIDS organization, single-subject preprocessing lock, and design variables are explicit, then export a protocol with source_claim_ids and report fields.
